rulings.Case Title: GEORGE VORPAHL ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Marshco Auto Parts, Incs Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Marshco Auto Parts, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. David F. Beach, Bishop Barry's motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant The McMichael Co. is continued on the court's own motion to September 27, 2017 to allow for supplemental filing. The motion does not comply with CRC 3.1362, specifically subdivisions (c), (d), and (e). Counsel may file supplemental papers that must comply with the entirety of CRC 3.1362. Supplemental filing due no later than September 21, 2017 at 4:00p.m. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 20, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carter was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant concedes that plaintiffs identified Henry "Tom" Havener as a witness regarding Mr. Carter's exposure to Bendix brakes. (UMF 4.) Defendant's failure to address Mr. Havener's knowledge precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to summary adjudication, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1 and 2 are sustained and objection no. 3 is overruled as to "No questions about Honeywell or Bendix were asked at the deposition and there was no Bendix identification by Mr. Orona" and sustained as to the remainder. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' evidence are sustained as to Exhibits C, F, I, J, K, M, S, T, V, W, Y, AA, DD, EE, FF, GG, HH, II, JJ, KK and NN and overruled as to Exhibits A, B, E, G, H, L, N, O, P, Q, R, U, X, Z, BB, CC, LL, MM, OO and PP. The Court considered plaintiffs' opposition as it does not violate CRC 2.108. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
